description.p: ZenNotes is a keyboard-first Markdown notes app that keeps your notes as ordinary Markdown files on disk — yours to own, sync, and version however you like.
On top of the files you already own, it adds:

ul.li: Vim-friendly editing with split and live-preview workflows
Wiki-links, tags, tasks, daily notes, and full-text search
Diagrams (Mermaid, TikZ), math (KaTeX), and syntax-highlighted code
CSV databases with Notion-style Table and Board views over plain .csv files
Archive, trash, quick capture, and a built-in command-line companion

screenshot.caption: Editing Markdown with live preview, sidebar, and tabs

description.p: Packaging-only patch: adds a native .rpm for Fedora, RHEL, and openSUSE (x64 and arm64) alongside the existing package formats. No application changes since 2.13.0.

Inline Excalidraw drawing embeds (![[drawing.excalidraw]]), a choice of where new drawings and databases are created, a confirmation toast after exporting a note to PDF, a toggle for a solid non-blinking cursor, and the Kanagawa Paper Ink theme, plus fixes for Excalidraw following custom themes, strikethrough crossing out math, editor focus when opening daily notes, and calendar task-list sync.

Custom Kanban statuses and a board for any inline @field, a two-way Vim clipboard so p/P paste from the system clipboard, and one-move clearing of selected tag filters, plus fixes for CircuiTikZ rendering on desktop, shortcuts and Vim keys after the app sits idle, keyboard focus returning to the editor when a pane closes, and drawing image export.
Task forwarding (the bullet-journal migrate gesture) and monthly notes, plus @time/@now and @ note-linking macros, o/O list continuation in Vim, :saveas, and a large batch of Vim and editor fixes.

Transclusion — embed a note's rendered content inline with ![[Note]] in the reading view and PDF export — plus backlinks for standard Markdown links, reorderable database columns, opt-in persistent self-host sessions, a CLI renamed to zn so it no longer clashes with Zen Browser, and nine fixes.

Bring your own theme — author full CSS themes, layer quick overrides on any theme, or recolor and resize with no-code Quick tweaks — plus the new Rosé Pine theme, per-vault view settings with synced workspaces, themed PDF export, and fixes across PDF export, tables, Vim, and keymaps.

A windowed sidebar that stays fast on huge vaults, a calmer redesigned Settings page, a new home view with your recent notes and today's tasks, Obsidian Excalidraw import, and image-rendering fixes in PDF export and Live Preview.
Link straight to your CSV databases with wikilinks, Notion-style slash commands in Quick Capture, and editor fixes (per-note undo, wikilinks in code, document outline).
A portable plain-text config file you can sync across machines, manual task reordering in notes and on the Tasks page, faster vault reopening, and remappable table navigation.
Real Markdown highlighting, system-wide quick capture on Wayland, manual note ordering, and the Kanagawa theme.
Maintenance release with editor and vault improvements.
Maintenance release with editor and vault improvements.
